seo_title: Your Body Is Constantly Replacing Itself
description: Dash points through a simplified body map as cells and organs refresh over time. A fast educational Short on renewal — skin, blood, and why you're not the same atoms you were years ago.
thumbnail_concept: Dash standing beside a simplified translucent body diagram; one organ glowing as it 'swaps'; bold text: 'YOUR BODY REPLACES ITSELF'; clean lab-teal palette, 9:16.
voice_direction: Bright classroom energy, playful disbelief on big numbers, precise on science words; humorous when Dash panics at 'you are under construction'.
music_direction: Clean ticking pulse (metronome-like renewal), soft mallet melody; hit accents when organs swap; warm resolve at end.
scientific_notes: Many tissues renew on different timescales (e.g., skin epidermis days–weeks; red blood cells ~120 days).
Avoid overprecise viral claims like 'new body every 7 years' as absolute — frame as continuous replacement of many parts at different rates.
Use simplified organ icons, not medical gore.

script.hook: Right now, your body is quietly replacing itself.
body: Not all at once — piece by piece.
Your skin cells renew on a short cycle, shedding the old and building the new.
Red blood cells last about four months… then fresh ones take the job.
Different organs refresh on different clocks.
You're less a fixed object — and more a living construction site.
script.payoff: So the 'you' watching this is already becoming the next you.
script.full_text: Right now, your body is quietly replacing itself. Not all at once — piece by piece. Your skin cells renew on a short cycle, shedding the old and building the new. Red blood cells last about four months… then fresh ones take the job. Different organs refresh on different clocks. You're less a fixed object — and more a living construction site. So the 'you' watching this is already becoming the next you.

scenes.narration: Right now, your body is quietly replacing itself.

background.design: Soft teal-gray gradient, faint geometric grid, floating cell icons drifting — not flat solid.

scenes.character_movement: Dash faces camera, recoils as a translucent body outline pops behind him.
scenes.image_prompt: Dash stick figure shocked in front of simplified translucent human body outline, soft lab teal gradient, educational 2D animation, 9:16

scenes.narration: Not all at once — piece by piece.

background.design: Body map splits into labeled tiles that pulse out of sync.

scenes.character_movement: Dash walks along the body map pointing at tiles lighting one-by-one.
scenes.image_prompt: Dash walking beside segmented simplified body diagram tiles pulsing, stick figure pointing, clean educational motion graphics background

scenes.narration: Your skin cells renew on a short cycle, shedding the old and building the new.

background.design: Cross-section skin layers as friendly geometric strata; old cells flake as particles.

scenes.character_movement: Dash climbs skin-layer diagram, uses magnifier; flakes drift past.
scenes.image_prompt: Dash climbing simplified skin cross-section diagram with magnifying glass, cells shedding as particles, stick figure mascot educational

scenes.narration: Red blood cells last about four months… then fresh ones take the job.

background.design: River of simplified disc cells; calendar mograph shows ~120 days.

scenes.character_movement: Dash runs beside blood-cell river holding a clock prop; throws fact card '~120 DAYS'.
scenes.image_prompt: Dash running beside simplified red blood cell river holding clock, fact card 120 days, playful educational stick figure animation

scenes.narration: Different organs refresh on different clocks.

background.design: Friendly organ icons on a vertical timeline; each swaps with a glow when Dash points.

scenes.character_movement: Dash points organ-to-organ; opens mini-doors revealing refreshed icon versions.
scenes.image_prompt: Dash pointing at simplified animated organ icons replacing themselves on a timeline, clean lab gradient, stick figure host

scenes.narration: You're less a fixed object — and more a living construction site.

background.design: Scaffolding lines over body silhouette; soft animated lighting; floating tools as metaphors (not literal surgery).

scenes.character_movement: Dash panics briefly at scaffolding, then thinks, then nods.
visuals: hard-hat optional as prop NOT worn as clothing redesign — held object only
scenes.image_prompt: Dash beside body silhouette with friendly scaffolding metaphor overlay, holding tiny hard hat prop, stick figure educational comedy beat

scenes.narration: So the 'you' watching this is already becoming the next you.

background.design: Two Dash silhouettes cross-dissolve in soft light; particle renewal field.

scenes.character_movement: Dash faces audience, celebrate jump; soft CTA.
scenes.image_prompt: Dash celebrating facing camera with soft renewal particle field and faint second silhouette dissolve, educational stick figure finale 9:16

caption_cues.text: Skin renews on a short cycle
